name: CEDS Finance
description_short: Colorado CDFI providing small business loans and Islamic-compliant financing to low-income, immigrant, refugee, and BIPOC entrepreneurs in Metro Denver and rural counties.
description_long:
CEDS Finance (Community Enterprise Development Services) was founded in 2012 as a nonprofit community development financial institution (CDFI) based in Aurora, Colorado. The organization's mission centers on supporting financial self-sufficiency for refugees, immigrants, and low-to-moderate income individuals seeking to start or strengthen businesses. Since its inception, CEDS has maintained a strong commitment to serving historically underserved populations, with 90% of its small business clients based in Denver and Aurora.

CEDS Finance offers two primary financing products: traditional interest-based small business loans with flexible terms up to $100,000, and Murabaha financing—Islamic-compliant financing also available up to $100,000. Beyond capital, the organization provides comprehensive free business support services including workshops, coaching, accounting assistance, marketing support, and business compliance guidance. Their staff speaks 16 languages, enabling culturally accessible service delivery across diverse communities. The organization also provides free workshops such as their "Access to Capital & Credit Building Workshop."

What distinguishes CEDS Finance is its specialization in underserved borrower populations and religious accommodation. The company explicitly markets itself as "the only Islamic compliant lender in Colorado," addressing a critical gap for Muslim entrepreneurs whose faith prohibits interest-bearing loans. The organization has earned Colorado's 2023 SBA Community Lender of the Year award and holds six-time CDFI Fund recognition, validating its track record of inclusive lending. Their geographic focus on both Metro Denver (Aurora specifically) and rural communities (Fort Morgan, Weld, and Morgan Counties) demonstrates commitment to underserved areas.

Honestly assessed, CEDS Finance operates within the constraints typical of nonprofit CDFIs: loan maximums of $100,000 may be insufficient for scaling businesses, and as a small nonprofit with approximately 11-50 employees, processing capacity may be limited. Their focus on specific demographic groups means they are not positioned as a general small business lender. The website does not display specific loan terms, APR rates, approval timelines, or detailed underwriting criteria, which are critical factors for borrowers evaluating options.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Only Islamic-compliant (Murabaha) lender in Colorado, addressing faith-based borrowing needs
Colorado's 2023 SBA Community Lender of the Year with six-time CDFI Fund recognition
Staff speaks 16 languages ensuring culturally accessible service
Free comprehensive business support including accounting, marketing, and compliance assistance
Serves low-income, immigrant, refugee, BIPOC, and Muslim business owners explicitly excluded by traditional lenders
Geographic focus includes underserved rural areas (Fort Morgan, Weld, Morgan Counties) alongside Denver metro
Flexible loan terms up to $100,000 with no rigid credit score requirements stated
cons: Loan maximum of $100,000 may be insufficient for growing or scaling businesses requiring larger capital
Website lacks specific loan terms, APR rates, approval timelines, and detailed underwriting criteria
Small nonprofit with ~20-50 employees may have limited processing capacity and longer turnaround times
Geographic service limited to Colorado (Metro Denver, Weld, Morgan Counties) only
No online application process described; requires in-person engagement in Aurora or service areas
best_for: Muslim entrepreneurs seeking Sharia-compliant Islamic financing without interest
Immigrant and refugee business owners facing discrimination or language barriers with traditional lenders
Low-income and BIPOC small business owners underserved by conventional bank lending
Rural Colorado entrepreneurs in Fort Morgan and Weld County seeking community lender support
diagnosis: CEDS Finance is best for entrepreneurs from immigrant, refugee, low-income, BIPOC, or Muslim communities in Colorado who value culturally competent lending and need free business support alongside capital. The main caveat is that loan amounts are capped at $100,000, making this unsuitable for businesses requiring larger growth capital, and service is geographically limited to Colorado only.
services: Small business loans up to $100,000 with flexible terms
Credit building and financial literacy resources
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 10660 E Colfax Ave Suite B, Aurora, CO 80010
